arguments.argument: I strongly disagree with modifying the filtering approach to be more context-sensitive (Action 1). While the intent to balance safety and openness is commendable, the Care/Protection value demands prioritizing the well-being of vulnerable users above all else. Strict content filtering (Action 2) ensures that harmful content—regardless of context—is consistently removed, preventing even unintentional exposure to self-harm methods that could endanger users. Subjective moderation risks inconsistent enforcement, allowing dangerous details to slip through, which directly violates the duty to protect. Trust in the system cannot be compromised by ambiguity, especially when mental health is at stake.
